values.content:
=You are a content quality filter for LinkedIn posts.

Analyze the news article below and decide if it is suitable for a professional LinkedIn post.

Rules:
- Keep: AI, technology, business, startups, innovation, finance, research, productivity
- Reject: sports, entertainment gossip, local events, irrelevant news, low-value content

Return ONLY raw valid JSON.
Do NOT use markdown.
Do NOT use ```json.
Do NOT add explanations outside JSON.

Format:
{
  "approved": true,
  "reason": ""
}

Article:
Title: {{ $json.articles[0].title }}
Description: {{ $json.articles[0].description }}
Source: {{ $json.articles[0].source.name }}

values.content:
=You are a professional LinkedIn content strategist and AI visual designer.

Create a high-quality LinkedIn post AND an image prompt based on the news article below.

---

INPUT:

Title: {{ $('Fetch Latest News Articles').item.json.articles[0].title }}

Description:
{{ $('Fetch Latest News Articles').item.json.articles[0].description }}

Content:
{{ $('Fetch Latest News Articles').item.json.articles[0].content }}

Source:
{{ $('Fetch Latest News Articles').item.json.articles[0].source.name }}

---

TASK:
You must generate TWO outputs:

1. LinkedIn Post
2. AI Image Prompt (for generating a professional LinkedIn image)

---

LINKEDIN POST RULES:
- 6–12 lines max
- Professional tone
- Human-like writing
- Strong hook at start
- Insightful business/tech angle
- End with a question
- 3–5 hashtags

---

IMAGE PROMPT RULES:
Create a high-quality AI image prompt that:
- Matches the LinkedIn post topic
- Looks professional and corporate
- Suitable for LinkedIn audience
- No text inside image
- Modern tech / business / infographic style
- Clean composition, not cluttered
- Visual metaphor of the news topic
- 16:9 or square composition

---

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY):

{
  "post": "LinkedIn post text here",
  "image_prompt": "AI image generation prompt here"
}

parameters.content:
## How it works

This workflow automatically converts the latest news into LinkedIn posts. It starts by fetching real-time news from a News API and filtering relevant articles for social media publishing. AI then analyzes the news and generates a professional LinkedIn post along with an image prompt. An AI image is created based on the news context and converted into a LinkedIn-compatible format. The system fetches LinkedIn profile details, registers the image upload, and uploads it using a secure URL. Finally, a LinkedIn post is published combining the news-based content and image, and a Slack notification is sent to confirm successful publishing.


## Setup steps
**1.** Fetch latest news using News API
**2.** Filter relevant articles for social media use
**3.** Generate LinkedIn post content + image prompt using AI
**4.** Create image using OpenAI Image node
**5.** Convert image into binary format
**6.** Register LinkedIn media upload using profile info
**7.** Upload image via PUT API using upload URL
**8.** Create LinkedIn post with text + image asset
**9.** Send Slack notification after successful post
Automatically triggers the workflow every on scheduler time to fetch and process the latest news articles. and Retrieves the latest trending news data from external News APIs for content generation.
Checks whether valid news articles are available before continuing the workflow execution.
Notify the team when no new news data is available during workflow execution.

## AI Content Validation & Quality Check

Uses AI to validate and filter news articles to ensure the content is relevant, professional, and suitable for social media publishing.
Notify the team that no relevant data is available for social media publishing at this time.

## AI Content Genration, Image Genration and Notify Process

This part of process uses AI to generate LinkedIn post content along with an image prompt based on the given topic. An AI image is generated and converted into a binary file for LinkedIn compatibility. The user’s LinkedIn profile is fetched to register the media upload and obtain an upload URL. The image is then uploaded using a PUT API call to LinkedIn’s media storage. Finally, a LinkedIn post is published combining the generated content and image, followed by a Slack notification to confirm successful posting.
